session.user:
Find where EnsurableResult and NonIdealStateMixin are defined in the erk codebase. Look for their class definitions and how other types implement them (like PushResult, PushError, BranchCreated, BranchAlreadyExists). I need to understand the patterns to implement similar types for submit_stack.
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Working directory: /home/runner/work/erk/erk
session.assistant: I'll help you find and understand EnsurableResult, NonIdealStateMixin, and how they're used in the codebase. Let me start by checking the documentation and then explore the code.
session.tool_result:
1Ôćĺ<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2Ôćĺ<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3Ôćĺ 4Ôćĺ# Agent Documentation 5Ôćĺ 6ÔćĺBefore starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7ÔćĺIf your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8Ôćĺ 9Ôćĺ## Categories 10Ôćĺ 11Ôćĺ- [architecture/](architecture/) ÔÇö Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). Add docs here for cross-cutting technical patterns. 12Ôćĺ- [capabilities/](capabilities/) 13Ôćĺ- [changelog/](changelog/) 14Ôćĺ- [checklists/](checklists/) 15Ôćĺ- [ci/](ci/) 16Ôćĺ- [claude-code/](claude-code/) 17Ôćĺ- [cli/](cli/) ÔÇö Explore when building CLI commands or output formatting. Add docs here for Click patterns and terminal UX. 18Ôćĺ- [commands/](commands/) ÔÇö Explore when creating or optimizing slash commands. Add docs here for command authoring patterns. 19Ôćĺ- [config/](config/) 20Ôćĺ- [configuration/](configuration/) 21Ôćĺ- [documentation/](documentation/) ÔÇö Explore when structuring or writing documentation. Add docs here for documentation methodology and best practices. 22Ôćĺ- [erk/](erk/) ÔÇö Explore when working with erk-specific workflows (worktrees, PR sync, Graphite). Add docs here for erk user-facing features. 23Ôćĺ- [erk-dev/](erk-dev/) 24Ôćĺ- [gateway/](gateway/) 25Ôćĺ- [hooks/](hooks/) ÔÇö Explore when creating or debugging hooks. Add docs here for hook development patterns. 26Ôćĺ- [integrations/](integrations/) 27Ôćĺ- [objectives/](objectives/) 28Ôćĺ- [planning/](planning/) ÔÇö Explore when working with plans, .erk/impl-context/ folders, or agent delegation. session.assistant: Perfect! I found a key document on discriminated union error handling. Let me read that and the architecture docs:
session.tool_result:
1Ôćĺ--- 2Ôćĺtitle: Discriminated Union Error Handling 3Ôćĺlast_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4Ôćĺaudit_result: clean 5Ôćĺread_when: 6Ôćĺ - "designing return types for operations that may fail" 7Ôćĺ - "implementing T | ErrorType patterns" 8Ôćĺ - "handling errors without exceptions" 9Ôćĺtripwires: 10Ôćĺ - action: "choosing between exceptions and discriminated unions for operation failures" 11Ôćĺ warning: "If callers branch on the error and continue the operation, use discriminated unions. If all callers just terminate and surface the message, use exceptions. Read the 'When to Use' section." 12Ôćĺ - action: "migrating a gateway method to return discriminated union" 13Ôćĺ warning: "Update all implementations (ABC, real, fake; add dry_run only for dry-run-participating gateways) AND all call sites AND tests. Incomplete migrations break type safety." 14Ôćĺ - action: "accessing properties on a discriminated union result without isinstance() check" 15Ôćĺ warning: "Always check isinstance(result, ErrorType) before accessing success-variant properties. Without type narrowing, you may access .message on a success type or .data on an error type." 16Ôćĺ - action: "using None as a success return value in a validation function (ErrorType | None where None = success)" 17Ôćĺ warning: "None-as-success is counterintuitive ÔÇö None typically signals absence/failure, not success. Use ValidThing | InvalidThing so both outcomes are explicit named types." 18Ôćĺ - action: "using if/else on a discriminated union without isinstance() for type narrowing" 19Ôćĺ warning: "Type checkers require isinstance() for narrowing. 'if result.is_error' or 'if not result' does not narrow. Use 'if isinstance(result, ErrorType):' for correct narrowing in both branches." 20Ôćĺ - action: "returning a collection (list, tuple, str) directly from a function with NonIdealState return type" 21Ôćĺ warning: "Raw built-ins can't inherit EnsurableResult. Wrap in a named frozen dataclass that inherits EnsurableResult and implements __iter__ if needed. See 'The Wrapping Rule' section." 22Ôćĺ - action: "using Protocol property descriptors on a frozen dataclass NonIdealState class" 23Ôćĺ warning: "Protocol property descriptors conflict with frozen dataclass fields. Use NonIdealStateMixin (at packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py) when the NonIdealState class uses dataclass fields." 24Ôćĺ - action: "placing @handle_non_ideal_exit before @click.command() or @click.pass_context" 25Ôćĺ warning: "@handle_non_ideal_exit must come AFTER @click.command() / @click.pass_context (outermost position in decorator stack). Inner position causes it to receive wrong arguments." 26Ôćĺ--- 27Ôćĺ 28Ôćĺ# Discriminated Union Error Handling 29Ôćĺ 30ÔćĺErk uses discriminated unions (`SuccessType | ErrorType`) at gateway and pipeline boundaries. Non-ideal states are **optional outcomes visible in the type signature** ÔÇö any reader (human or AI agent) can see from `T | ErrorType` what can happen and write control flow accordingly. This is the primary advantage over exceptions: **Python exceptions are not knowable from the type signature**, making them hostile to agent-driven development and harder for both humans and agents to reason about.
... [396 lines omitted] ...
32Ôćĺ## The Core Trade-off: Unions vs Exceptions 34Ôćĺ**Use discriminated unions when the caller continues after failure** ÔÇö branching logic, multiple error types, or inspection of error fields (like `pr_number`, `branch_name`). 36Ôćĺ**Use exceptions when all callers terminate identically** ÔÇö error just surfaces as a message, no branching logic, no meaningful field inspection. 38Ôćĺ### When Exceptions Are Better: The Worktree Operations Case 40ÔćĺWorktree add/remove failures are _expected_ (path collisions, missing branches), but they're still better as exceptions because no caller does anything beyond extracting the message and terminating: 46Ôćĺexcept RuntimeError as e: 47Ôćĺ raise UserFacingCliError(str(e)) 50ÔćĺThe exception pattern is simpler here: no caller branches on error content, no caller inspects error structure, and every call site terminates identically. A discriminated union would add ceremony without value. 54ÔćĺContrast with `create_branch` and `submit_branch`, where callers _do_ branch on error types: 72Ôćĺ1. **Branching logic** ÔÇö different handling for "already exists" vs other failures 74Ôćĺ3. **Type-safe continuation** ÔÇö caller keeps running after handling the error 78ÔćĺGateway methods return `SuccessType | ErrorType` where: 81Ôćĺ- **Error types** are frozen dataclasses with `message: str` and `error_type` property 83Ôćĺ<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/types.py, PushResult, PushError --> 85ÔćĺSee `PushResult` and `PushError` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/types.py`. 90Ôćĺif isinstance(push_result, PushError): 91Ôćĺ raise UserFacingCliError(push_result.message) 101Ôćĺ- **Error types** (`NonIdealState`): `.ensure()` raises `NonIdealStateError` 105Ôćĺ# One-liner: raises if error, returns the value if success 116Ôćĺdef _ensure_comments(result: list[IssueComment] | GitHubAPIFailed) -> list[IssueComment]: 117Ôćĺ if isinstance(result, GitHubAPIFailed): 118Ôćĺ exit_with_error(result.error_type, result.message) 119Ôćĺ assert not isinstance(result, GitHubAPIFailed) 131Ôćĺdef issue_comments(...) -> list[IssueComment] | GitHubAPIFailed: ... 146Ôćĺ| All error paths should raise (caller can't recover) | `.ensure()` one-liner | 147Ôćĺ| Caller branches on success vs error | `isinstance()` check | 148Ôćĺ| Caller branches on multiple error types | `isinstance()` checks per type | 154Ôćĺ# isinstance() ÔÇö when caller handles the error path explicitly 156Ôćĺif isinstance(result, GitHubAPIFailed): 157Ôćĺ log_warning(result.message) 170Ôćĺ- **`NonIdealStateMixin`**: Provides `.ensure()` for error types that use `message: str` as a field (frozen dataclass field conflicts with `NonIdealState` protocol's `@property message`). Used by `GitHubAPIFailed`. 171Ôćĺ- **`NonIdealState`**: Protocol for error types with computed `message` properties. `.ensure()` raises `NonIdealStateError`. 175Ôćĺ<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/ensure.py, UserFacingCliError --> 177ÔćĺCLI commands use `UserFacingCliError` for error propagation. See `UserFacingCliError` in `src/erk/cli/ensure.py`. 180Ôćĺ# Check error case first and raise 182Ôćĺif isinstance(result, PushError): 183Ôćĺ raise UserFacingCliError(result.message) 187Ôćĺ**Why `UserFacingCliError`:** 189Ôćĺ- Caught at CLI entry point (`main()`) with consistent error styling 191Ôćĺ- One-line pattern replaces verbose `user_output(error) + raise SystemExit(1)` 193Ôćĺ## Pipeline Discriminated Unions: Structured Error Propagation 195ÔćĺBoth submit and land pipelines use discriminated unions to thread errors through multi-step operations: 197Ôćĺ<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py, SubmitState, SubmitError --> 198Ôćĺ<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/land_pipeline.py, LandState, LandError --> 200ÔćĺSee `SubmitError` in `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py` and `LandError` in `src/erk/cli/commands/land_pipeline.py`. 205ÔćĺPipelineStep = Callable[[ErkContext, State], State | Error] 211Ôćĺ- `Error` to short-circuit pipeline 213Ôćĺ**Why structured error types?** 215Ôćĺ1. **Phase tracking** ÔÇö caller knows if error occurred in validation vs execution 216Ôćĺ2. **Machine-readable `error_type`** ÔÇö enables specific error handling (e.g., "pr-checks-failing" vs "merge-conflict") 220Ôćĺ**Why `dict[str, str]` for details?** Not `dict[str, Any]` or `dict[str, str] | None`. Every error has details (even if empty `{}`), and string values enable consistent serialization/logging without type uncertainty. 224Ôćĺdef run_validation_pipeline(ctx: ErkContext, state: State) -> State | Error: 225Ôćĺ """Run all validation steps, short-circuit on first error.""" 228Ôćĺ if isinstance(result, Error): 242Ôćĺ """Returns True on success, error message string on failure.""" 247Ôćĺ error_detail = merge_result if isinstance(merge_result, str) else "Unknown" 248Ôćĺ return f"Failed to merge: {error_detail}" 253Ôćĺ<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py, MergeResult, MergeError --> 255ÔćĺSee `MergeResult` and `MergeError` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py`. 258Ôćĺdef merge_pr(self, repo_root: Path, pr_number: int, ...) -> MergeResult | MergeError: 259Ôćĺ """Returns MergeResult on success, MergeError on failure.""" 263Ôćĺif isinstance(merge_result, MergeError): 264Ôćĺ return LandError( 266Ôćĺ error_type="merge-failed", 267Ôćĺ message=f"Failed to merge PR #{pr_number}\n\n{merge_result.message}", 279Ôćĺ5. Update tests to check `isinstance(result, ErrorType)` 287ÔćĺError types implement the `NonIdealState` protocol. See `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py`. 291Ôćĺ- `error_type` property (read-only, machine-readable classification) 294ÔćĺThis enables generic error handling without coupling to specific error types: 297Ôćĺdef handle_error(result: Any) -> str: 305Ôćĺ### Error Types Are Frozen Dataclasses 307Ôćĺ<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/types.py, PushError --> 309ÔćĺAll error types use `@dataclass(frozen=True)` for immutability. See `PushError` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/types.py` for the canonical pattern: a single `message: str` field and an `error_type` property returning a kebab-case string like `"push-failed"`. 313ÔćĺError types should carry domain-meaningful fields for caller inspection: 317ÔćĺSee `PRNotFound` in `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py`. It carries `pr_number: int | None` (set when looking up by number) and `branch: str | None` (set when looking up by branch), enabling callers to construct better error messages or branch on specific failure modes. 323Ôćĺ| `<Operation>Error` | `SubmitError`, `MergeError` | Operation failed | 331Ôćĺ### vs. Exceptions 333Ôćĺ| Discriminated Unions | Exceptions | 344Ôćĺ| Preserves error context | Loses information | 346Ôćĺ| Multiple error types possible | Single failure state | 354Ôćĺdef fetch() -> Data | Error: ... 357Ôćĺdef fetch() -> Result[Data, Error]: ... 372Ôćĺ# T | ErrorType: isinstance() required for type narrowing 374Ôćĺif isinstance(result, FetchError): 375Ôćĺ handle_error(result.message) 382Ôćĺ**Anti-pattern:** `validate_thing() -> ErrorType | None` where `None` means success. 384ÔćĺThis is counterintuitive because `None` typically signals absence or failure, not success. Callers end up writing `if result is not None:` to check for errors, which reads backwards. 394Ôćĺerror = validate_slug(slug) 395Ôćĺif error is not None: # "if not nothing" = error? 396Ôćĺ handle_error(error) 411Ôćĺ handle_error(result)

1Ôćĺ--- 2Ôćĺtitle: EnsureIdeal Pattern for Type Narrowing 3Ôćĺread_when: 4Ôćĺ - "handling discriminated union returns in CLI commands" 5Ôćĺ - "narrowing types from T | NonIdealState or T | ErrorType" 6Ôćĺ - "working with PR lookups, branch detection, or API calls that return union types" 7Ôćĺ - "seeing EnsureIdeal in code and wondering when to use it vs Ensure" 8Ôćĺtripwires: 9Ôćĺ - action: "using EnsureIdeal for discriminated union narrowing" 10Ôćĺ warning: "Only use when the error type implements NonIdealState protocol OR provides a message field. For custom error types without standard fields, add a specific EnsureIdeal method." 11Ôćĺ - action: "choosing between Ensure and EnsureIdeal" 12Ôćĺ warning: "Ensure is for invariant checks (preconditions). EnsureIdeal is for type narrowing (handling operations that can return non-ideal states). If the value comes from an operation that returns T | ErrorType, use EnsureIdeal." 13Ôćĺlast_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 14Ôćĺaudit_result: edited 15Ôćĺ--- 16Ôćĺ 17Ôćĺ# EnsureIdeal Pattern for Type Narrowing 18Ôćĺ 19Ôćĺ`EnsureIdeal` provides CLI-level type narrowing for gateway operations that return discriminated unions. It's a specialized tool for the validation phase of CLI commands, converting `T | ErrorType` into `T` or terminating with styled error output. 20Ôćĺ 21Ôćĺ## Why Two Classes? 22Ôćĺ 23ÔćĺThe separation between `Ensure` and `EnsureIdeal` reflects two distinct validation concerns: 24Ôćĺ 25Ôćĺ| Class | Validates | Source of Values | Failure Meaning | 26Ôćĺ| ------------- | ---------------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------------- | 27Ôćĺ| `Ensure` | Invariants and preconditions | Arguments, config, local state | Programming error or user input error | 28Ôćĺ| `EnsureIdeal` | Gateway operation results | I/O operations, API calls | Expected external failure | 29Ôćĺ 30Ôćĺ**Decision rule**: If the value originates from a gateway method returning `T | ErrorType`, use `EnsureIdeal`. If it's an argument check, config validation, or state assertion, use `Ensure`.
... [91 lines omitted] ...
34Ôćĺ`Ensure.invariant(not isinstance(result, PRNotFound), "...")` mixes type checking with error handling, bypassing the type narrowing that Python's `isinstance()` provides. `EnsureIdeal` methods explicitly handle the error type and return the narrowed success type, giving you both error handling AND type safety. 44ÔćĺUse with gateway methods returning `PRDetails | PRNotFound`. Call `EnsureIdeal.unwrap_pr(result, message)` where you supply the error message because `PRNotFound` is a minimal sentinel without a `message` field. 52ÔćĺUse with `GitHubChecks` methods returning `PRDetails | NoPRForBranch | PRNotFoundError`. Call `EnsureIdeal.pr(result)` with no message parameter ÔÇö the error types implement `NonIdealState` protocol which includes a `message` property. 66Ôćĺ1. **Gateway returns a discriminated union** ÔÇö not an exception, not a boolean 68Ôćĺ3. **Error type has a `message` field or implements `NonIdealState`** ÔÇö otherwise the pattern doesn't fit 75Ôćĺ- **Exception-based errors** ÔÇö those bypass type narrowing entirely 77Ôćĺ- **Error types without messages** ÔÇö either add `unwrap_*` pattern (like PR) or refactor the error type 81Ôćĺ`EnsureIdeal` is a Phase 1 (validation) tool. It terminates on failure, so it must run _before_ any mutations occur. 104Ôćĺ1. **Type check**: `isinstance(result, ErrorType)` 105Ôćĺ2. **Styled output**: `user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + message)` 110ÔćĺWhy `SystemExit`? It exits immediately without stack traces, which is appropriate for expected failures like "PR not found". These aren't bugs ÔÇö they're operational conditions that should present cleanly to users. 114Ôćĺ- [Discriminated Union Error Handling](../architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md) ÔÇö When to use unions vs exceptions

/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py
session.assistant: Perfect! Now let me read these core files:
session.tool_result:
1Ôćĺ"""Non-ideal state types for operations that can fail gracefully. 2Ôćĺ 3ÔćĺThis module provides: 4Ôćĺ- NonIdealState: Marker interface for error states (Protocol) 5Ôćĺ- NonIdealStateError: Exception raised by NonIdealState.ensure() 6Ôćĺ- Specific error classes: BranchDetectionFailed, NoPRForBranch, etc. 7Ôćĺ 8ÔćĺThe NonIdealState pattern allows functions to return T | NonIdealState, 9Ôćĺletting callers decide how to handle errors (exit, log, retry, etc.) 10Ôćĺrather than forcing early exit. 11Ôćĺ 12ÔćĺUsage: 13Ôćĺ from erk_shared.non_ideal_state import NonIdealState, BranchDetectionFailed 14Ôćĺ 15Ôćĺ # Check if result is a non-ideal state 16Ôćĺ result = some_operation() 17Ôćĺ if isinstance(result, NonIdealState): 18Ôćĺ print(f"Error: {result.message}") 19Ôćĺ 20Ôćĺ # Or raise immediately 21Ôćĺ result = some_operation() 22Ôćĺ if isinstance(result, NonIdealState): 23Ôćĺ result.ensure() 24Ôćĺ""" 25Ôćĺ 26Ôćĺfrom __future__ import annotations 27Ôćĺ 28Ôćĺfrom dataclasses import dataclass 29Ôćĺfrom typing import NoReturn, Protocol, Self, runtime_checkable 30Ôćĺ
... [130 lines omitted] ...
32Ôćĺclass NonIdealStateError(Exception): 36Ôćĺ self.error_type = state.error_type 49Ôćĺ raise NonIdealStateError(self) # type: ignore[arg-type] 57Ôćĺ - If result is a NonIdealState (error): raises NonIdealStateError 73Ôćĺ All NonIdealState implementations must provide error_type and message 74Ôćĺ properties for consistent error handling. 78Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: ... 84Ôćĺ """Raise NonIdealStateError with this state's details.""" 85Ôćĺ raise NonIdealStateError(self) 89Ôćĺclass BranchDetectionFailed(NonIdealState): 93Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 94Ôćĺ return "branch-detection-failed" 108Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 117Ôćĺclass PRNotFoundError(NonIdealState): 123Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 132Ôćĺclass GitHubAPIFailed(NonIdealStateMixin): 133Ôćĺ """GitHub API call failed with an error.""" 138Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 139Ôćĺ return "github-api-failed" 149Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str:

1Ôćĺ"""Discriminated union types for Git remote operations. 2Ôćĺ 3ÔćĺPushResult | PushError and PullRebaseResult | PullRebaseError follow 4Ôćĺthe NonIdealState pattern established by MergeResult | MergeError. 5Ôćĺ""" 6Ôćĺ 7Ôćĺfrom dataclasses import dataclass 8Ôćĺ 9Ôćĺ 10Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 11Ôćĺclass PushResult: 12Ôćĺ """Success result from pushing to remote.""" 13Ôćĺ 14Ôćĺ 15Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 16Ôćĺclass PushError: 17Ôćĺ """Error result from pushing to remote. Implements NonIdealState.""" 18Ôćĺ 19Ôćĺ message: str 20Ôćĺ 21Ôćĺ @property 22Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 23Ôćĺ return "push-failed" 24Ôćĺ 25Ôćĺ 26Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 27Ôćĺclass PullRebaseResult: 28Ôćĺ """Success result from pull --rebase.""" 29Ôćĺ 30Ôćĺ
... [15 lines omitted] ...
32Ôćĺclass PullRebaseError: 33Ôćĺ """Error result from pull --rebase. Implements NonIdealState.""" 38Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 39Ôćĺ return "pull-rebase-failed"

1Ôćĺ"""Discriminated union types for Git branch operations. 2Ôćĺ 3ÔćĺBranchCreated | BranchAlreadyExists follows the NonIdealState pattern 4Ôćĺestablished by PushResult | PushError in remote_ops/types.py. 5Ôćĺ""" 6Ôćĺ 7Ôćĺfrom dataclasses import dataclass 8Ôćĺ 9Ôćĺ 10Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 11Ôćĺclass BranchCreated: 12Ôćĺ """Success result from creating a branch.""" 13Ôćĺ 14Ôćĺ 15Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 16Ôćĺclass BranchAlreadyExists: 17Ôćĺ """Error: branch already exists. Implements NonIdealState.""" 18Ôćĺ 19Ôćĺ branch_name: str 20Ôćĺ message: str 21Ôćĺ 22Ôćĺ @property 23Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 24Ôćĺ return "branch-already-exists" 25Ôćĺ

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

1Ôćĺ"""Type definitions for GitHub operations.""" 2Ôćĺ 3Ôćĺfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field 4Ôćĺfrom datetime import UTC, datetime 5Ôćĺfrom pathlib import Path 6Ôćĺfrom typing import Any, Literal, TypedDict 7Ôćĺ 8Ôćĺfrom erk_shared.non_ideal_state import EnsurableResult 9Ôćĺ 10ÔćĺPRState = Literal["OPEN", "MERGED", "CLOSED"] 11Ôćĺ 12ÔćĺPRReviewState = Literal["PENDING", "COMMENTED", "APPROVED", "CHANGES_REQUESTED", "DISMISSED"] 13Ôćĺ 14ÔćĺMergeableStatus = Literal["MERGEABLE", "CONFLICTING", "UNKNOWN"] 15Ôćĺ 16Ôćĺ 17Ôćĺclass StatusCheckRollupData(TypedDict, total=False): 18Ôćĺ state: str 19Ôćĺ contexts: dict[str, Any] 20Ôćĺ 21Ôćĺ 22Ôćĺdef _epoch_sentinel() -> datetime: 23Ôćĺ """Return the epoch sentinel datetime used as default for missing PR timestamps.""" 24Ôćĺ return datetime(2000, 1, 1, tzinfo=UTC) 25Ôćĺ 26Ôćĺ 27Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 28Ôćĺclass BodyText: 29Ôćĺ """Body content as inline text. 30Ôćĺ
... [401 lines omitted] ...
116Ôćĺ conclusion: Result when completed ("success", "failure", "cancelled", etc.). 135Ôćĺclass MergeError: 136Ôćĺ """Error result from merging a PR. Implements NonIdealState.""" 142Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 143Ôćĺ return "merge-failed" 150Ôćĺ Used as part of union return types for LBYL-style error handling. 224ÔćĺWorkflowRunConclusion = Literal["success", "failure", "cancelled", "skipped"] 261Ôćĺ Raises AttributeError when accessed as a string, preventing silent bugs 271Ôćĺ raise AttributeError(msg) 282Ôćĺ raise AttributeError(msg) 302Ôćĺ Accessing unavailable fields raises AttributeError. 352Ôćĺ raise AttributeError(msg) 356Ôćĺ raise AttributeError(msg) 379Ôćĺ str(value) # Trigger the error 391Ôćĺ str(value) # Trigger the error

1Ôćĺ"""Branch metadata dataclass for Graphite integration.""" 2Ôćĺ 3Ôćĺfrom __future__ import annotations 4Ôćĺ 5Ôćĺimport secrets 6Ôćĺfrom dataclasses import dataclass 7Ôćĺ 8Ôćĺ 9Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 10Ôćĺclass BranchMetadata: 11Ôćĺ """Metadata for a single gt-tracked branch. 12Ôćĺ 13Ôćĺ This is used by the gt commands to provide machine-readable branch information. 14Ôćĺ 15Ôćĺ Attributes: 16Ôćĺ name: Branch name 17Ôćĺ parent: Parent branch name, or None for trunk 18Ôćĺ children: List of child branch names 19Ôćĺ is_trunk: True if this is the trunk branch (main/master) 20Ôćĺ commit_sha: Actual git commit SHA from git branch head 21Ôćĺ tracked_revision: Graphite's cached branchRevision from .graphite_cache_persist 22Ôćĺ (may differ from commit_sha after rebase/restack) 23Ôćĺ """ 24Ôćĺ 25Ôćĺ name: str 26Ôćĺ parent: str | None 27Ôćĺ children: list[str] 28Ôćĺ is_trunk: bool 29Ôćĺ commit_sha: str | None 30Ôćĺ tracked_revision: str | None = None

1Ôćĺ# Plan: Eliminate Exceptions from Graphite `submit_stack` (Objective #9324, Phase 1) 2Ôćĺ 3Ôćĺ## Context 4Ôćĺ 5ÔćĺObjective #9324 aims to eliminate exceptions as control flow from gateway methods. Phase 1 targets `submit_stack` in the Graphite gateway ÔÇö the method currently raises `RuntimeError` on failure, and callers parse error message strings to distinguish failure modes (restack required, nothing to submit, generic failure). This is fragile and not self-documenting. 6Ôćĺ 7ÔćĺThe codebase already has a well-established discriminated union pattern (e.g., `PushResult | PushError`, `BranchCreated | BranchAlreadyExists`) using frozen dataclasses with `NonIdealState` protocol and `EnsurableResult` mixin. 8Ôćĺ 9Ôćĺ## Node 1.1: Define Discriminated Union Types 10Ôćĺ 11Ôćĺ**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/types.py` (add to existing file) 12Ôćĺ 13ÔćĺDefine 4 types to eliminate all string parsing at call sites: 14Ôćĺ 15Ôćĺ```python 16Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 17Ôćĺclass SubmitStackResult(EnsurableResult): 18Ôćĺ """Stack submitted successfully.""" 19Ôćĺ 20Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 21Ôćĺclass SubmitStackNothingToSubmit: 22Ôćĺ """Benign: stack had no changes to submit. Callers may treat as success.""" 23Ôćĺ 24Ôćĺ@dataclass(frozen=True) 25Ôćĺclass SubmitStackRestackRequired(NonIdealStateMixin): 26Ôćĺ """Stack has conflicts requiring manual restack before submit.""" 27Ôćĺ message: str 28Ôćĺ 29Ôćĺ @property 30Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str:
... [118 lines omitted] ...
34Ôćĺclass SubmitStackError(NonIdealStateMixin): 35Ôćĺ """Generic submit_stack failure (timeout, exit code, etc.).""" 39Ôćĺ def error_type(self) -> str: 40Ôćĺ return "submit-failed" 43Ôćĺ**Why 4 types instead of 2:** Call sites currently branch on 3 distinct outcomes beyond success: (a) "restack" in error Ôćĺ custom user guidance, (b) "nothing to submit" Ôćĺ treat as success, (c) generic failure. Separate types make these branches type-safe with no string parsing. 47ÔćĺSubmitStackOutcome = SubmitStackResult | SubmitStackNothingToSubmit | SubmitStackRestackRequired | SubmitStackError 54Ôćĺ- Remove `Raises: RuntimeError` from docstring 57Ôćĺ- Remove `raise RuntimeError(...)` calls 58Ôćĺ- Convert subprocess exceptions to return values: 59Ôćĺ - `TimeoutExpired` Ôćĺ `SubmitStackError(message="gt submit timed out...")` 60Ôćĺ - `CalledProcessError` with "restack" in stderr Ôćĺ `SubmitStackRestackRequired(message=...)` 61Ôćĺ - `CalledProcessError` with "nothing to submit"/"no changes" in stderr Ôćĺ `SubmitStackNothingToSubmit()` 62Ôćĺ - `CalledProcessError` other Ôćĺ `SubmitStackError(message=...)` 66Ôćĺ- Change `submit_stack_raises: Exception | None` constructor param to `submit_stack_result: SubmitStackOutcome` defaulting to `SubmitStackResult()` 68Ôćĺ- Update all test call sites that pass `submit_stack_raises=RuntimeError(...)`: 69Ôćĺ - `test_graphite_first_flow.py:94` Ôćĺ `submit_stack_result=SubmitStackError(message="gt submit failed")` 71Ôćĺ - `test_enhance_with_graphite.py:151` Ôćĺ `submit_stack_result=SubmitStackError(message="network timeout")` 73Ôćĺ - `fake_ops.py:531,640` Ôćĺ convert error param to appropriate result type 79Ôćĺ- Return `SubmitStackError(message=f"Graphite is disabled: {self.reason}")` instead of raising `GraphiteDisabledError` 80Ôćĺ- **Note:** This changes behavior ÔÇö disabled sentinel currently raises a different exception type. Callers that catch `GraphiteDisabledError` separately need checking. Search for `GraphiteDisabledError` catch blocks. 90Ôćĺ return SubmitError(phase="push_and_create_pr", error_type="graphite_restack_required", ...) 91Ôćĺif isinstance(result, (SubmitStackError, SubmitStackNothingToSubmit)): 92Ôćĺ return SubmitError(phase="push_and_create_pr", error_type="graphite_submit_failed", ...) 104Ôćĺif isinstance(result, (SubmitStackRestackRequired, SubmitStackError)): 105Ôćĺ return SubmitError(phase="enhance_with_graphite", error_type="graphite_enhance_failed", ...) 110Ôćĺ# Before: try/except converting to SubmitBranchError 115Ôćĺreturn SubmitBranchError(message=result.message if hasattr(result, 'message') else "Unknown error") 126Ôćĺ| `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/disabled.py` | Return error instead of raising |

100Ôćĺ 101Ôćĺ Runs `gt auth` and parses the output to determine authentication status. 102Ôćĺ This is a LBYL check to validate Graphite authentication before operations 103Ôćĺ that require it (like gt submit). 104Ôćĺ 105Ôćĺ Returns: 106Ôćĺ Tuple of (is_authenticated, username, repo_info): 107Ôćĺ - is_authenticated: True if gt is authenticated 108Ôćĺ - username: Authenticated username (e.g., "schrockn") or None if not authenticated 109Ôćĺ - repo_info: Repository info string (e.g., "dagster-io/erk") or None 110Ôćĺ 111Ôćĺ Example: 112Ôćĺ >>> graphite.check_auth_status() 113Ôćĺ (True, "schrockn", "dagster-io/erk") 114Ôćĺ >>> # If not authenticated: 115Ôćĺ (False, None, None) 116Ôćĺ """ 117Ôćĺ ... 118Ôćĺ 119Ôćĺ @abstractmethod 120Ôćĺ def squash_branch(self, repo_root: Path, *, quiet: bool) -> None: 121Ôćĺ """Squash all commits on the current branch into one. 122Ôćĺ 123Ôćĺ Uses `gt squash` to consolidate commits. This is typically called 124Ôćĺ before submitting a PR to create a clean single-commit branch. 125Ôćĺ 126Ôćĺ Args: 127Ôćĺ repo_root: Repository root directory 128Ôćĺ quiet: If True, suppress output 129Ôćĺ
... [75 lines omitted] ...
131Ôćĺ RuntimeError: If gt squash fails 159Ôćĺ RuntimeError: If gt submit fails or times out

200Ôćĺ 201Ôćĺ # Extract repo info from "Ready to submit PRs to OWNER/REPO" 202Ôćĺ repo_info: str | None = None 203Ôćĺ for line in output.split("\n"): 204Ôćĺ if "Ready to submit PRs to" in line: 205Ôćĺ parts = line.split("Ready to submit PRs to") 206Ôćĺ if len(parts) >= 2: 207Ôćĺ repo_info = parts[1].strip() 208Ôćĺ break 209Ôćĺ 210Ôćĺ return (True, username, repo_info) 211Ôćĺ 212Ôćĺ def squash_branch(self, repo_root: Path, *, quiet: bool) -> None: 213Ôćĺ """Squash all commits on the current branch into one.""" 214Ôćĺ cmd = ["gt", "squash", "--no-edit", "--no-interactive"] 215Ôćĺ 216Ôćĺ run_subprocess_with_context( 217Ôćĺ cmd=cmd, 218Ôćĺ operation_context="squash branch commits with Graphite", 219Ôćĺ cwd=repo_root, 220Ôćĺ stdout=DEVNULL if quiet else sys.stdout, 221Ôćĺ stderr=subprocess.PIPE, 222Ôćĺ ) 223Ôćĺ 224Ôćĺ def submit_stack( 225Ôćĺ self, 226Ôćĺ repo_root: Path, 227Ôćĺ *, 228Ôćĺ publish: bool, 229Ôćĺ restack: bool,
... [75 lines omitted] ...
264Ôćĺ raise RuntimeError( 267Ôćĺ except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: 270Ôćĺ raise RuntimeError( 271Ôćĺ f"gt submit failed (exit code {e.returncode}): {e.stderr or ''}" 288Ôćĺ return (False, result.stderr.strip() or "gt restack failed") 294Ôćĺ means the branch is tracked, non-zero means untracked or error.

session.tool_result: 324: ctx.graphite.submit_stack( 325- state.repo_root, 326- publish=True, 327- restack=False, 328- quiet=state.quiet, 329- force=effective_force, 330- ) 331- except RuntimeError as e: 332- error_str = str(e) 333- if "restack" in error_str.lower(): 334- return SubmitError( -- 757: ctx.graphite.submit_stack( 758- repo_root, 759- publish=True, 760- restack=False, 761- quiet=state.quiet, 762- force=state.force, 763- ) 764- except RuntimeError as e: 765- error_msg = str(e).lower() 766- if "nothing to submit" in error_msg or "no changes" in error_msg: 767- click.echo(click.style(" PR already up to date with Graphite", fg="green"))
session.assistant:
Excellent! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary document for the user:
## Summary: EnsurableResult, NonIdealStateMixin, and Discriminated Union Pattern
Based on my exploration of the erk codebase, here's what I found about the pattern you need to implement for submit_stack:
### Core Architecture: Three Key Base Classes/Protocols
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py`**
This file contains the foundational types for discriminated union error handling:
1. **`NonIdealStateError(Exception)`** ÔÇö The exception raised by `.ensure()` on error types 2. **`NonIdealStateMixin`** ÔÇö Mixin class for error types with `message: str` as a frozen dataclass field 3. **`EnsurableResult`** ÔÇö Mixin class for success types that provides `.ensure()` returning self 4. **`NonIdealState`** ÔÇö Protocol defining the interface for all error types (requires `error_type` and `message` properties)
### Pattern Usage: Real Examples in Codebase
**1. Simple Success/Error Pairs (like your submit_stack case)**
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/types.py`** ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class PushResult: """Success result from pushing to remote.""" # Empty marker class ÔÇö operation itself (successful push) is the signal
@dataclass(frozen=True) class PushError: """Error result from pushing to remote. Implements NonIdealState.""" message: str
@property def error_type(self) -> str: return "push-failed" ```
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/types.py`** ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class BranchCreated: """Success result from creating a branch."""
@dataclass(frozen=True) class BranchAlreadyExists: """Error: branch already exists. Implements NonIdealState.""" branch_name: str # Domain-specific field message: str
@property def error_type(self) -> str: return "branch-already-exists" ```
**2. More Complex: Success with Data (implements `EnsurableResult`)**
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py`** ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class MergeResult: """Success result from merging a PR.""" pr_number: int # Carries domain context
@dataclass(frozen=True) class MergeError: """Error result from merging a PR. Implements NonIdealState.""" pr_number: int message: str
@property def error_type(self) -> str: return "merge-failed" ```
**3. Wrapped Collections (implements `EnsurableResult`)**
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/types.py`** ```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class IssueComments(EnsurableResult): """Wrapper for tuple[IssueComment, ...] to enable .ensure() unwrapping.""" comments: tuple[IssueComment, ...]
def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[IssueComment]: return iter(self.comments) ```
This pattern allows: - One-liner unwrapping: `result.ensure()` - Type-safe branching: `if isinstance(result, ErrorType):` - No string parsing at call sites
### Your Case: submit_stack Discriminated Union
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.erk/impl-context/plnd--O9324-submit-stack-discrim-03-21-1132/plan.md`** (contains complete implementation plan)
The plan defines 4 types for submit_stack to eliminate string parsing in two call sites:
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class SubmitStackResult(EnsurableResult): """Stack submitted successfully."""
@dataclass(frozen=True) class SubmitStackNothingToSubmit: """Benign: stack had no changes to submit. Callers may treat as success."""
@dataclass(frozen=True) class SubmitStackRestackRequired(NonIdealStateMixin): """Stack has conflicts requiring manual restack before submit.""" message: str
@property def error_type(self) -> str: return "restack-required"
@dataclass(frozen=True) class SubmitStackError(NonIdealStateMixin): """Generic submit_stack failure (timeout, exit code, etc.).""" message: str
@property def error_type(self) -> str: return "submit-failed"
# Type alias SubmitStackOutcome = ( SubmitStackResult | SubmitStackNothingToSubmit | SubmitStackRestackRequired | SubmitStackError ) ```
### Key Design Decisions
1. **Use `EnsurableResult` for:** - Success types in `T | ErrorType` unions - Enables `.ensure()` that returns self - Used when callers will call `.ensure()` directly
2. **Use `NonIdealStateMixin` for:** - Error types with `message: str` as a frozen dataclass field - Provides `.ensure()` that raises `NonIdealStateError` - Avoids descriptor conflicts with `NonIdealState` protocol properties
3. **Use bare frozen dataclass for:** - Non-error variants that aren't success or failure (like `SubmitStackNothingToSubmit`) - Sentinel/marker types
4. **Include domain context fields:** - `BranchAlreadyExists` carries `branch_name` - `MergeError` carries `pr_number` - Enables callers to construct better error messages without additional lookups
5. **Four submit_stack types instead of two:** - Current code branches on three distinct non-success outcomes via string parsing - Separate types make these branches type-safe: `SubmitStackRestackRequired` vs `SubmitStackNothingToSubmit` vs `SubmitStackError`
### How Call Sites Currently Parse Errors
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:323-350`** ```python try: ctx.graphite.submit_stack(...) except RuntimeError as e: error_str = str(e) if "restack" in error_str.lower(): # ÔćÉ String parsing! return SubmitError(...) ```
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/submit_pipeline.py:757-767`** ```python try: ctx.graphite.submit_stack(...) except RuntimeError as e: error_msg = str(e).lower() if "nothing to submit" in error_msg or "no changes" in error_msg: # ÔćÉ String parsing! click.echo("PR already up to date...") ```
### Current Exception Raising (to be replaced)
**File: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/real.py:224-276`** ```python def submit_stack(...) -> None: try: result = subprocess.run(...) except subprocess.TimeoutExpired as e: raise RuntimeError("gt submit timed out after 90 seconds...") # Ôćĺ SubmitStackError except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e: raise RuntimeError(f"gt submit failed (exit code {e.returncode}): {e.stderr or ''}") # Logic needs to check if "restack" in stderr Ôćĺ SubmitStackRestackRequired # Check if "nothing to submit"/"no changes" in stderr Ôćĺ SubmitStackNothingToSubmit # Otherwise Ôćĺ SubmitStackError ```
### Related Documentation References
- **[Discriminated Union Error Handling](file:///home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/discriminated-union-error-handling.md)** ÔÇö Comprehensive pattern explanation - **[EnsureIdeal Pattern](file:///home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/cli/ensure-ideal-pattern.md)** ÔÇö CLI-level type narrowing - **[Gateway ABC Implementation](file:///home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md)** ÔÇö 4-place implementation pattern (ABC, Real, Fake, DryRun, etc.)
### File Locations
| Purpose | File Path | |---------|-----------| | Core mixins/protocols | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py` | | Git remote ops types | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/remote_ops/types.py` | | Git branch ops types | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/git/branch_ops/types.py` | | GitHub types | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/types.py` | | GitHub issues types | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/issues/types.py` | | Graphite gateway ABC | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/abc.py` | | Graphite real impl | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/graphite/real.py` | | Implementation plan | `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.erk/impl-context/plnd--O9324-submit-stack-discrim-03-21-1132/plan.md` |
This should give you everything you need to implement similar types for your submit_stack work!
